item.title: p38 MAP kinase senses short-chain fatty acids to attenuate Toll-like receptor signaling and intestinal inflammation.
item.description: Science advances - Toll-like receptor (TLR) signaling is critical for innate immune system. However, whether it is directly modulated by microbiota-derived metabolites remains unclear. Here, we show that the short-ch...

item.title: Metabolome remodeling with reverse-engineered exclusive enteral nutrition in children with active Crohn's disease.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - For children with Crohn's disease (CD), dietary therapy with exclusive enteral nutrition (EEN) is effective in achieving clinical and biochemical remission. We investigated changes in metabolites i...

item.title: Assessment of Malnutrition in Crohn's Disease Patients: A Novel Risk Prediction Model with Dynamic Optimization Potential and Effectiveness Validation.
item.description: Journal of visualized experiments : JoVE - This study aimed to construct and validate a malnutrition risk prediction model combining multivariable logistic regression and machine learning for patients with Crohn's disease (CD), with the goa...
